model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: SMU holds a commanding advantage in predictive metrics, ranking 42nd in both KenPom and Torvik compared to Miami (OH) at 93rd and 87th respectively, with an adjusted efficiency margin nearly 10 points higher (18.1 vs 8.3). The return of star guard BJ Edwards from an ankle injury that sidelined him for the last five games significantly boosts SMU's outlook, as the team's late-season struggles were largely attributed to his absence. While Miami (OH)'s 31-1 record is impressive, their schedule was among the weakest in the nation, and their lone loss in the MAC tournament quarterfinals to UMass exposed vulnerabilities that SMU's ACC-tested roster should be able to exploit with their elite offense (AdjO rank 27) against Miami (OH)'s below-average defense (AdjD rank 149).

reasoningStep.title: SMU vs Miami (OH) – First Four Play-In Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: SMU's significant advantages in adjusted efficiency metrics, conference strength, and the return of injured star BJ Edwards make them a clear favorite over a Miami (OH) team whose gaudy 31-1 record was built against one of the nation's weakest schedules.
evidence: SMU KenPom #42 (AdjEM +18.11, AdjO 123.0, AdjD 104.8) vs Miami OH KenPom #93 (AdjEM +8.25, AdjO 116.8, AdjD 108.6)
SMU Torvik #42 vs Miami OH Torvik #87 — nearly 10-point efficiency gap
BJ Edwards confirmed returning for NCAA Tournament after missing last 5 games with ankle injury (ESPN/Pete Thamel)
Miami OH went 31-0 in regular season but lost to UMass 87-83 in MAC tournament quarterfinals; played one of nation's weakest schedules
SMU 20-13 in ACC (power conference) vs Miami OH 31-1 in MAC (mid-major with weak SOS)

modelTrace.content:
This is very helpful. Key findings:

1. **SMU (20-13, ACC)**: KenPom #42, Torvik #42. Strong offense (AdjO ~123, rank 27) but mediocre defense (AdjD ~104.8, rank 95). Star guard BJ Edwards missed last 5 games with ankle injury but **is set to return for the NCAA Tournament** per ESPN's Pete Thamel.

2. **Miami OH (31-1, MAC)**: KenPom #93, Torvik #87. Went 31-0 in regular season but lost in MAC tournament quarterfinals to UMass. Got in as an at-large/last team. Weaker metrics (AdjO ~116.8, AdjD ~108.6) due to very weak schedule strength.

The gap is significant: SMU's adjusted efficiency margin is +18.11 (KenPom) vs Miami OH's +8.25. That's nearly a 10-point gap. SMU also gets BJ Edwards back, which was the key factor in their late-season slide. Despite Miami OH's impressive record, their schedule was one of the weakest in the country, and their metrics reflect that.
